title: Graphein ChartSpec
description: A single JSON-serializable Graphein chart, slicer, or dashboard specification, discriminated on `type`. Generated from the TypeScript types in packages/core/src/spec — edit the types and run `npm run gen:schema`.

field.description: Source column to aggregate (omit only for `count`).

as.description: Output column receiving the aggregated value.

AggregateTransform.description: Group rows by `groupby` and summarize each group into a single output row. Omitting `groupby` collapses the whole dataset into one row.

type.description: What to draw. Omit to infer: a `line` when `value` is set, a `band` when `from`/`to` are set. `zone` is a band — a semantic alias for a threshold band. A `point` marks a single (`x`, `y`) data coordinate with a labeled dot.

axis.description: Which axis the value(s) are measured against. `y` (default) draws a horizontal line / full-width band; `x` draws a vertical line / full-height band.

value.description: Reference value for a `line` (number, category, or date — matching the axis).

from.description: Start of the span for a `band`/`zone`.

to.description: End of the span for a `band`/`zone`.

x.description: X coordinate of a `point` callout (a data value on the x-axis).

y.description: Y coordinate of a `point` callout (a data value on the y-axis).

markerRadius.description: Marker radius in pixels for a `point` (default 3.5).

label.description: Short text label drawn beside the annotation.

color.description: Stroke (line) / fill (band) color. Defaults to a muted theme color.

strokeWidth.description: Line width in pixels for a `line` (default 1.5).

strokeDash.description: Dash pattern for the stroke; `[]` is solid. Lines/bands default to dashed.

labelPosition.description: Where the label anchors along the annotation (default `end`).

Annotation.description:
A reference annotation overlaid on a cartesian chart: a single reference
**line** at a value, or a filled **band** / threshold **zone** between two values. Useful for targets, goals, averages, control limits, and "danger" ranges. Drawn over the gridlines and data marks; labels render in the overlay.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

annotations.description: Reference lines, bands, and threshold zones overlaid on the plot.

insights.description: Auto-mark notable data points (`true` = max + min). See InsightOptions.

trendline.description: Overlay a linear line of best fit. See TrendlineConfig.

facet.description: Split into a trellis grid of small multiples. See FacetConfig.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

group.description: Group series side-by-side (default when `series` present and not stacked).

annotations.description: Reference lines, bands, and threshold zones overlaid on the plot.

insights.description: Auto-mark notable data points (`true` = top + bottom category). See InsightOptions.

facet.description: Split into a trellis grid of small multiples. See FacetConfig.

as.description: Output column(s). A single name receives the bin **start**; a `[start, end]` pair receives both edges (handy for histogram bars and range labels).

maxbins.description: Target bin count; a "nice" step approximates it. Default 10.

step.description: Explicit bin width. Overrides `maxbins` when set.

extent.description: Restrict binning to this `[min, max]`; values outside get a `null` bin.

nice.description: Snap the bin domain to round numbers (default true).

BinTransform.description: Bucket a quantitative `bin` field into evenly sized bins. Drives the histogram chart and any "distribution" view without pre-binning in user code.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

encoding.description: `x` is the category axis (one box per category); `y` holds the raw observations that are summarized into quartiles. Add `series` to draw grouped boxes side-by-side within each category.

whisker.description:
Whisker rule:
- 'tukey' (default): whiskers reach the furthest points within 1.5×IQR of the quartiles; points beyond are drawn as outliers.
- 'minMax': whiskers span the full data range (no outliers).

outliers.description: Draw outlier points beyond the whiskers (tukey only; default true).

annotations.description: Reference lines, bands, and threshold zones overlaid on the plot.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

value.description: The featured measure (literal or a field, optionally aggregated).

target.description: Target/comparative marker (literal or field) drawn as a vertical tick.

ranges.description: Qualitative range boundaries on the scale (e.g. [50, 75, 100]).

label.description: Caption to the left of the bar (defaults to the spec title or value field).

format.description: Number format for the value + axis ticks.

BulletSpec.description: Bullet graph — a compact linear KPI-vs-target: a measure bar over qualitative `ranges` (poor/ok/good background bands) with a `target` comparative marker. The featured value/target are literals or fields (optionally aggregated).

CalculateTransform.description: Derive a new column from a **safe expression** evaluated per row, writing the result to `as`. The expression language supports arithmetic, comparison, logical and ternary operators, string concatenation, member access (`datum['my field']`), and a whitelist of functions (e.g. `round`, `lower`, `if`, `coalesce`). Bare identifiers reference row columns. It is parsed to an AST and evaluated with **no `eval`/`Function`** and no access to globals.

date.description: Date field (Date or ISO string) → one cell per day.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

scheme.description: Sequential color scheme name for the value scale (default 'teal').

CalendarHeatmapSpec.description: Calendar heatmap — a GitHub-contributions-style grid of one cell per day, colored by a value via a sequential scale, with weekday rows and month labels. Pass tidy rows of `{ date, value }`; dates may be `Date` objects or ISO strings.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

geo.description: Map geometry: a GeoJSON FeatureCollection of Polygon/MultiPolygon features.

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

encoding.description: `key` joins each data row to a feature; `color` is the numeric value that drives the fill via a sequential color scale.

featureId.description: Where to read a feature's join id: a property name (e.g. 'name' for `feature.properties.name'), or 'id' for the top-level `feature.id`. Defaults to trying `feature.id`, then `properties.id`, then `properties.name`.

mark.description: Which mark to draw for this layer.

encoding.description: The measure (and optional per-layer formatting) plotted on `y`.

axis.description: Which y-axis this layer is measured against. Defaults to `left`.

area.description: Fill under the line (line layers — equivalent to an `area` mark).

name.description: Legend label for the layer. Defaults to the y field's title or name.

color.description: Override the layer's color (otherwise drawn from the theme palette).

ComboLayer.description: One layer of a ComboSpec: a mark plus the measure it plots on `y`. All layers share the combo's `x`. A layer can be measured against the primary (`left`) y-axis or an independent secondary (`right`) axis — the basis of a dual-axis bar+line chart.

ComboMark.description: The mark a single combo layer draws.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

ComboSpec.description: Combo / dual-axis chart: composes multiple cartesian ComboLayer s over a shared `x` axis — the canonical BI "bar + line" view. Layers may target a primary (`left`) or secondary (`right`) y-axis, each with its own independent scale. Bars require a categorical `x`; lines/areas/points align to category centers.

cols.description: Number of grid columns at full width (default 12).

rowHeight.description: Height of one grid row in px (default 96).

breakpoints.description: Responsive column counts. When the dashboard is narrower than a breakpoint's `maxWidth`, the grid switches to that breakpoint's `cols` and tiles reflow (DataZen-style). The smallest matching breakpoint wins. Defaults to `[{ maxWidth: 600, cols: 1 }, { maxWidth: 960, cols: 6 }]`.

navigators.description:
Where unplaced slicers are laid out:
- 'top' (default): compact slicers (dropdown/search/range/dateRange) form a navigator strip above the grid, like a BI filter bar.
- 'inline': every slicer is laid out in the grid like a chart.

sections.description: Optional section bands; unlisted views render in an implicit trailing section.

preset.description: Placement preset for unplaced views. Defaults to 'auto'.

maxWidth.description: Constrain and center the dashboard page.

density.description: Spacing preset applied before explicit gap/rowHeight overrides.

padding.description: Page padding in px. Defaults to a value derived from gap.

maxWidth.description: Dashboard/section width threshold in px. Smallest matching maxWidth wins.

w.description: Override column span at this width.

h.description: Override row span at this width.

hidden.description: Hide the view at this width.

id.description: Optional stable id for the section band.

title.description: Section title shown above the grid.

subtitle.description: Muted line shown under the section title.

views.description: View ids included in this section.

background.description: Header band background tint.

collapsed.description: Initial collapsed state; click the header to expand/collapse.

data.description: Shared dataset; views without their own `data` inherit this.

subtitle.description: Optional muted subtitle shown under the title in the dashboard header.

params.description: Dashboard-level named selections (e.g. seeded initial values).

interactions.description:
Cross-interaction policy:
- 'auto' (default): every source cross-filters the page — slicers and chart clicks both subset rows in the other views; the clicked chart dims its own unpicked marks instead of hiding them.
- 'none': views are laid out but not linked.
- an array of InteractionLink: explicit wiring (replaces auto).

id.description: Unique id within the dashboard (used for layout + link references).

spec.description: The chart or slicer to render. Inherits the dashboard's `data` when omitted.

x.description: Grid column start (1-based). Omit for auto-flow placement.

y.description: Grid row start (1-based). Omit for auto-flow placement.

w.description: Column span (in grid columns). Defaults by view type.

h.description: Row span (in grid rows). Defaults by view type.

title.description: Optional card title drawn by the dashboard chrome.

subtitle.description: Optional card subtitle drawn below the dashboard card title.

frame.description: Whether to draw the card frame. Defaults to true.

background.description: Card background override.

accent.description: Solid accent bar color for the card.

padding.description: Card content padding. Defaults to 'standard'.

responsive.description: Per-view responsive span overrides; smallest matching maxWidth wins.

DashboardView.description: One placed view in a dashboard: a chart or slicer spec plus grid placement.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

field.description: The data field this slicer reads its options/bounds from and filters on.

param.description: The param name this slicer publishes to. Defaults to `field`, which makes a slicer auto-wire to any visual filtering/highlighting on that param name.

label.description: Label shown above the control. Defaults to `title` or the field name.

as.description: How consumers should react: emphasize matches or subset rows. Default 'filter'.

presets.description: Show relative presets (last 7 / 30 / 90 days, all). Default true.

format.description: Date format hint for the value labels (e.g. '%b %e, %Y').

DateRangeSlicerSpec.description: A temporal min/max range over a date field, with relative presets.

Datum.description: A single data record (row). Agent-supplied data is an array of these.

width.description: Fixed width in px. Omit for responsive (fills container).

height.description: Fixed height in px. Omit for responsive (fills container).

autoResize.description: Re-render on container resize (default true when width/height omitted).

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

field.description: The data field this slicer reads its options/bounds from and filters on.

param.description: The param name this slicer publishes to. Defaults to `field`, which makes a slicer auto-wire to any visual filtering/highlighting on that param name.

label.description: Label shown above the control. Defaults to `title` or the field name.

as.description: How consumers should react: emphasize matches or subset rows. Default 'filter'.

multiple.description: Allow choosing multiple values (emits a set). Default false (single-select).

placeholder.description: Placeholder shown when nothing is selected.

DropdownSlicerSpec.description: Choose one (single) or several (multi) of a field's distinct values.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

category.description: The category for each row (one band down the y-axis).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

group.description: The group each dot belongs to (2+ levels → one dot each, connected).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

labels.description: Value labels beside the dots (default false).

format.description: Number format for value labels and the x-axis.

sort.description: Order the category rows (default: data order). `gap` sorts by dot spread.

DumbbellSpec.description: Dumbbell / connected dot plot — for each `category`, a dot per `group` placed on a shared horizontal value axis and joined by a connector, so the gap between groups (e.g. before vs. after, male vs. female) reads directly. Categories run down the y-axis. Pass tidy rows of `{ category, value, group }`.

field.description: The field whose distinct values become one panel each.

columns.description: Number of grid columns. Defaults to roughly √n, capped for readability.

sort.description: Order the panels by their facet value (default `ascending`).

FacetConfig.description: **Faceting** (small multiples): split a chart into a trellis grid of panels, one per distinct value of a field, all sharing identical x/y/color scales so the panels are directly comparable. Enable via a chart's `facet` field — a single field reference yields a whole comparison grid, which is why it is so agent-friendly. Supported on `line`, `area`, `bar`, and `scatter`.

type.description: Data type; inferred from the data when omitted.

aggregate.description: Optional aggregation applied when grouping (e.g. sum of sales).

format.description: Number/date format hint (see format module), e.g. ',.0f' or '%b %Y'.

FieldDef.description: How a data field maps onto a visual channel.

FieldType.description:
Encoding channel data types (Vega-Lite-like):
- quantitative: continuous numbers (linear/log scales)
- temporal: dates/times (time scale)
- ordinal: ordered categories
- nominal: unordered categories

FilterClause.description: One clause of a `filter`: a named param or a literal predicate.

FilterPredicate.description: A declarative, JSON predicate for FilterTransform. Leaf predicates test one `field`; `and` / `or` / `not` compose them. Comparisons coerce numerically (numbers first, then dates), so temporal bounds work as ISO strings.

FoldTransform.description: Gather several columns into key/value rows (wide → long). Each input row is repeated once per folded column. Use it to turn a wide table into a tidy one a `series` channel can split.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

labels.description: Show stage labels + value/percent inside the funnel (default true).

percent.description:
What the per-stage percentage is measured against:
- 'first' (default): share retained vs. the top of the funnel.
- 'previous': step conversion vs. the stage above.

FunnelSpec.description: A funnel chart: ordered `stage`s drawn as stacked, centered trapezoids whose width encodes `value`. Ideal for conversion / drop-off across a pipeline. Each stage shows its value and the share retained relative to the first (or the previous) stage.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

value.description: The measured value (literal or a field, optionally aggregated over data).

target.description: Optional target/threshold marker drawn as a needle/tick.

label.description: Caption under the value (defaults to the spec title or value field).

format.description: Number format for the value + scale ticks (e.g. ',.0f', '.0%').

bands.description: Qualitative arc bands, each filling the scale up to `to`.

GaugeSpec.description: Gauge — a radial dial showing a single value against a `[min, max]` scale, with an optional `target` needle and qualitative background `bands`. The value is a literal or a field (optionally aggregated, like KpiSpec ).

coordinates.description: Rings: the first is the exterior, the rest are holes.

GeoPosition.description: A GeoJSON position: [longitude, latitude] (or projected [x, y]).

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

param.description: The param whose current value drives the emphasis.

HighlightConfig.description: Reference a named selection for highlighting.

maxbins.description: Target bin count (approximate — a "nice" step is chosen). Default 10.

extent.description: Restrict binning to `[min, max]`; values outside are dropped.

nice.description: Snap bin edges to nice round numbers (default true).

HistogramBin.description: Binning controls for a HistogramSpec (mirror the `bin` transform).

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

encoding.description: `x` is the quantitative field to bin (also carries the axis title/format).

bin.description: Binning controls (default ~10 nice bins, or an explicit `step`/`extent`).

density.description: Normalize bar heights to a probability density (area sums to 1).

color.description: Bar colour (defaults to the first theme palette colour).

HistogramSpec.description: Histogram: bins a single quantitative field and draws the per-bin frequency as gapless bars. Binning happens inside the chart (reusing the `bin` transform), so an agent passes raw observations — no manual pre-binning. Bar height is the bin count by default, or a probability density (area sums to 1) when `density:true`.

outliers.description: Mark statistical outliers beyond the 1.5×IQR fences (default false).

InsightOptions.description: Which automatic data insights to mark on a cartesian plot. Enable via a chart's `insights` field — `true` marks the max and min; an object opts into specific callouts. The library derives the points and draws labeled markers, so an agent never has to reason out (or hardcode) where the peak is.

source.description: Source view id (the visual whose selection drives the interaction).

target.description: Target view id(s), or '*' for every other view.

as.description: How targets react. Default: 'filter' for slicers, 'highlight' for charts.

fields.description: Identity fields to match on. Defaults to the source's key field(s).

InteractionLink.description: Explicit cross-interaction from one view to others (overrides auto-wiring).

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

value.description: Literal value or a field (optionally aggregated over data).

delta.description: Delta vs. a comparison, drives the up/down indicator.

sparkline.description: Inline sparkline from a numeric field.

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

points.description: Show point markers on the line.

area.description: Fill the area under the line.

annotations.description: Reference lines, bands, and threshold zones overlaid on the plot.

insights.description: Auto-mark notable data points (`true` = max + min). See InsightOptions.

trendline.description: Overlay a linear line of best fit. See TrendlineConfig.

facet.description: Split into a trellis grid of small multiples. See FacetConfig.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

field.description: The data field this slicer reads its options/bounds from and filters on.

param.description: The param name this slicer publishes to. Defaults to `field`, which makes a slicer auto-wire to any visual filtering/highlighting on that param name.

label.description: Label shown above the control. Defaults to `title` or the field name.

as.description: How consumers should react: emphasize matches or subset rows. Default 'filter'.

searchThreshold.description: Show a search-within box once options exceed this count (default 8).

ListSlicerSpec.description: A scrollable checkbox list of a field's distinct values (multi-select).

LiteralPredicate.description: A literal, param-free predicate usable directly in `filter`. Each form maps to a SelectionValue, so the same matcher handles params and literals.

MapProjection.description:
How feature coordinates are mapped to the screen:
- 'mercator' / 'equirectangular': geographic [lon, lat] projections.
- 'identity': coordinates are already planar [x, y] in screen orientation (y increases downward), e.g. data pre-projected with Albers USA. Useful for composite projections or non-geographic polygon maps.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

placement.description:
Where labels sit:
- 'auto' (default): inside the slice when the text fits, otherwise an outside callout with a leader line.
- 'inside': always inside (small slices are dropped if they don't fit).
- 'outside': always an outside callout with a leader line.

content.description: What each label says (default: 'percent' inside, 'category-percent' for outside callouts).

minShare.description: Hide labels for slices below this share of the total (0..1, default 0.01).

connector.description: Leader-line colour for outside callouts: the slice colour or a muted grey.

PieLabels.description: Fine-grained control over pie/donut slice labels.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

donut.description: true for a default donut, or a 0..1 inner-radius ratio.

labels.description: Value/percent labels. `true` (default) / `false` toggles them; pass a PieLabels object to control placement (inside vs. outside callouts), what each label says, and the connector style.

tuples.description: Each tuple is one selected combination, aligned to `fields`.

PointSelection.description: Discrete picks: rows whose `fields` equal one of the selected `tuples`.

RangeSelection.description: A continuous span over `field` (numeric or temporal). Bounds are inclusive.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

field.description: The data field this slicer reads its options/bounds from and filters on.

param.description: The param name this slicer publishes to. Defaults to `field`, which makes a slicer auto-wire to any visual filtering/highlighting on that param name.

label.description: Label shown above the control. Defaults to `title` or the field name.

as.description: How consumers should react: emphasize matches or subset rows. Default 'filter'.

min.description: Lower bound; defaults to the data minimum of `field`.

max.description: Upper bound; defaults to the data maximum of `field`.

step.description: Thumb step; defaults to a sensible fraction of the range.

format.description: Number format hint for the value labels (e.g. ',.0f').

RangeSlicerSpec.description: A numeric min/max range over a quantitative field (dual-thumb slider).

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

encoding.description: Each data row is one link. Nodes are derived from the distinct `source` and `target` values; `value` is the flow magnitude (link/node thickness).

nodePadding.description: Vertical gap between stacked nodes in px (default 14).

nodeValues.description: Show the node total alongside its label (default true).

domain.description: Explicit domain; numbers for continuous, strings for categorical.

nice.description: Round the domain to nice values (continuous only).

zero.description: Force the domain to include zero (continuous only).

padding.description: Band/point padding (0..1).

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

annotations.description: Reference lines, bands, and threshold zones overlaid on the plot.

trendline.description: Overlay a linear line of best fit. See TrendlineConfig.

facet.description: Split into a trellis grid of small multiples. See FacetConfig.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

field.description: The data field this slicer reads its options/bounds from and filters on.

param.description: The param name this slicer publishes to. Defaults to `field`, which makes a slicer auto-wire to any visual filtering/highlighting on that param name.

label.description: Label shown above the control. Defaults to `title` or the field name.

as.description: How consumers should react: emphasize matches or subset rows. Default 'filter'.

debounce.description: Debounce in ms before publishing the query (default 200).

SearchSlicerSpec.description: A debounced case-insensitive substring filter over a text field.

type.description:
- `point`: discrete picks (click marks, choose categories). Toggling builds a set of selected tuples.
- `interval`: a continuous range (brush on an axis, a min/max slider, a date range).

on.description: Pointer event that updates the selection. Default `'click'`.

fields.description: Data fields that identify the selection. Defaults to the chart's key channel (e.g. the `x`/`series` field for cartesian charts, the `field` of a slicer).

toggle.description: Click an already-selected mark to deselect, and accumulate multiple picks. Default `true` for `on:'click'`.

empty.description: What an *empty* selection means for consumers: `'all'` (the default) treats "nothing selected" as "match everything" (no dimming / no filtering); `'none'` treats it as "match nothing".

SelectionDef.description: How a selection is captured from user interaction.

name.description: Unique name within a chart or dashboard.

value.description: Optional initial value.

SelectionParam.description: A named selection a visual defines.

SelectionValue.description: The resolved value of a selection — what lives in the store.

SetSelection.description: Membership: rows whose `field` value is one of `values` (dropdown / list).

bowing.description: How much straight strokes bow between their endpoints.

fillStyle.description: Fill treatment for closed marks (bars, wedges, cells). Default 'hachure'.

hachureGap.description: Spacing (px) between hachure lines. Omit to derive from stroke width.

seed.description: Explicit PRNG seed; omit to derive a stable one from the spec.

font.description: Apply the hand-drawn font to titles/labels/axes (default true).

SketchConfig.description: Hand-drawn ("sketch") rendering options. Set `sketch: true` on any spec for the defaults, or pass this object to tune the look. All fields are plain JSON so specs still round-trip through `JSON.stringify`.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

labels.description: Direct labels (series name + value) at each line's ends (default true).

colorByChange.description: Color each line green/red by its net rise/fall instead of by series.

format.description: Number format for value labels and the y-axis.

SlopeSpec.description: Slope graph — a minimal "before/after" chart: each `series` is a line joining its `y` value across two (or a few) ordinal `x` positions, with direct end labels instead of a legend. Reads change-in-rank and rise/fall at a glance. Pass tidy rows of `{ x, y, series }` (one row per series per x position).

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

columns.description: Explicit columns; inferred from data keys when omitted.

striped.description: Zebra striping (off by default — flat aesthetic).

TextSelection.description: A case-insensitive substring match over `field` (search box).

background.description: Page/plot background.

surface.description: Cards / elevated surfaces (KPI cards, tooltips, table headers).

ThemeColors.description: Graphein design tokens. Flat, modern aesthetic: solid fills, minimal shadows, restrained radii, and a teal accent. Two built-in themes (light/dark). Token values are literals so this module stays dependency-free; charts combine these with the color module for derived ramps and contrast.

TimeUnit.description: Calendar unit a TimeUnitTransform truncates a timestamp to.

TimeUnitTransform.description: Truncate a temporal `field` to the start of a calendar unit (e.g. month), writing a `Date` to `as`. Lets agents aggregate by period without date math.

Transform.description: A single declarative transform step. Distinguished by its operator key (`aggregate` / `bin` / `filter` / `fold` / `timeUnit` / `calculate`); a step carries exactly one. Applied in array order by applyTransforms.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

group.description: Optional parent grouping → nested parent tiles (one level deep).

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

scheme.description: Sequential color scheme name when `color` is numeric (default 'teal').

labels.description: Show the value beneath the label inside each tile (default true).

TreemapSpec.description: Treemap — part-to-whole as nested rectangles sized by a measure. Pass tidy rows (one per leaf); the area of each tile is proportional to `value`. An optional `group` field nests leaves under parent tiles (one level), and `color` overrides the default per-group/leaf fill (numeric → sequential scale, categorical → palette).

groupBy.description: Fit a separate line per color/series group. Defaults to `true` when the chart splits into multiple series, `false` (one overall fit) otherwise.

label.description: Draw an `R²=…` label at the end of each fitted line (default false).

color.description: Line color. Defaults to the series color (grouped) or a muted ink.

TrendlineConfig.description: A derived **trendline** (line of best fit) overlaid on a cartesian plot. Enable via a chart's `trendline` field — `true` fits a single linear regression; an object configures the fit and its styling. The library computes the regression from the plotted rows, so an agent never has to derive slope/intercept coordinates by hand. Requires a continuous or temporal x-axis (scatter, line, area) — it is meaningless on a categorical/band axis.

data.description: Row-oriented (tidy) data. Required for all charts/tables.

transform.description: Declarative data transforms applied to `data` (in order) before the chart is built — aggregate, bin, filter, fold, timeUnit. Reshape data *inside* the spec instead of pre-massaging the array, so encodings can reference fields the pipeline produces. Pure JSON; see Transform.

theme.description: Theme name ('light' | 'dark') or a partial override object.

description.description: Accessible description (alt text) for the chart. Used verbatim as the chart's `aria-label`; when omitted, a concise label is synthesized from the type, title, and data. Agents should set this to convey the chart's intent.

padding.description: Extra padding around the plot area.

background.description: Background override (defaults to the theme background).

sketch.description: Render the chart in a hand-drawn ("sketch") style — wobbly outlines, hachure fills, and a handwriting font. `true` uses the defaults; pass a `SketchConfig` to tune it. Omit (or `false`) for the default clean rendering.

params.description: Named selections this visual publishes. Clicking marks, brushing, or changing a slicer updates the param's value on the shared selection store, which other visuals can consume via BaseSpec.highlight or BaseSpec.filter. Pure data — no callbacks.

highlight.description: Emphasize rows matching a selection and dim the rest. References a param by name (typically published by another visual for cross-highlighting). An array unions several sources: a row is emphasized if it matches *any* active one.

filter.description: Subset this visual's rows to those matching every clause (logical AND). Each clause is a named param (cross-filter) or a literal predicate.

stage.description: Funnel stage (ordered category, one bar per stage).

value.description: Magnitude channel (Sankey link value / generic measure).

y2.description: Upper bound for ranged area / band marks.

color.description: Color channel (series color or continuous color).

series.description: Splits data into multiple series (multi-line, grouped/stacked bars).

key.description: Geographic key matching a GeoJSON feature (choropleth).

totals.description: Stage labels to draw as absolute running-total bars from the baseline.

showTotal.description: Append a final bar summing every step (default false).

totalLabel.description: Label for the appended total bar (default 'Total').

labels.description: Show the per-bar value labels (default true).

increaseColor.description: Color for upward steps (default theme positive/green).

decreaseColor.description: Color for downward steps (default theme negative/red).

totalColor.description: Color for total/subtotal bars (default theme accent).

WaterfallSpec.description: Waterfall — a column chart of signed steps where each bar floats from the running total to its new level, showing how sequential increases and decreases build to a final value. Each `value` is the **signed change** at that stage (positive rises, negative falls). Stages named in `totals` (and an optional appended grand total) draw as absolute bars from the baseline. Increases, decreases, and totals are colored distinctly and joined by connector lines.
